mission.title: coredns: Missing Docker image for 1.11.3
mission.description: Missing Docker image for 1.11.3. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify coredns troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your coredns deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns -n coredns --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review coredns configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant coredns configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl get configmap -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=coredns
```
The docker image for CoreDNS release 1.11.3 is not available due to persistent issues in the CoreDNS release process.
Once these issue are resolved, the image will be made available.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Missing Docker image for 1.11.3
steps.description:
, I had some time a last week to try to work out migrating to docker build/push actions to enable publishing images to both docker and github's container repo. I didn't get very far. I was unable to get the multi-arch build to work completely.

Due to shifting work priorities and personal events, I will not have time to look into this further for the next couple of months.

One way someone can help would be to test/debug a docker release in a coredns fork to a personal docker account to see if

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Missing Docker image for 1.11.3 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns -n coredns --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n coredns --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
, I had some time a last week to try to work out migrating to docker build/push actions to enable publishing images to both docker and github's container repo. I didn't get very far. I was unable to get the multi-arch build to work completely.

Due to shifting work priorities and personal events, I will not have time to look into this further for the next couple of months.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with coredns installed or the issue environment reproducible.
